message:
feat(store,cli): add collector health, freshness, and drift detection (bd-new.1)

Three new tables (collector_health, machine_baselines, drift_events)
with full CRUD, freshness summaries with stale detection, z-score based
drift detection against machine baselines, and CLI subcommands
(vc health freshness/collectors/drift/baselines). Includes 20 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -62,7 +62,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-69d","title":"Implement afsc (Automated Flywheel Setup Checker) collector","description":"Implement collector for afsc (Automated Flywheel Setup Checker) - flywheel setup health monitoring.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker status --format json
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker list --format jsonl
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker validate --format jsonl
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker classify-error --format jsonl
- Use CLI Incremental Window pattern (time-bounded)

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE afsc_status_snapshot(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_health TEXT,
    installers_total INTEGER,
    installers_healthy INTEGER,
    last_run_at TIMESTAMP,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE afsc_run_facts(
    machine_id TEXT,
    run_id TEXT,
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    status TEXT,
    duration_ms BIGINT,
    error_category TEXT,
    installer_name TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, run_id)
);

CREATE TABLE afsc_event_logs(
    machine_id TEXT,
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    event_type TEXT,
    severity TEXT,
    message TEXT,
    installer_name TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE afsc_error_clusters(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    error_category TEXT,
    occurrence_count INTEGER,
    last_seen TIMESTAMP,
    example_errors_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, error_category)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement AfscCollector with Collector trait
- Parse status output for overall health summary
- Track run history with success/failure status
- Cluster errors by category for pattern detection
- Handle missing afsc binary gracefully

UI VALUE:
- Flywheel setup health dashboard
- Installer success rates
- Error pattern analysis
- Setup drift detection

TESTS:
- Mock afsc CLI outputs
- Verify error clustering
- Test incremental collection

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-7b7","title":"Implement cloud_benchmarker collector","description":"Implement collector for cloud_benchmarker - VPS machine performance baseline and drift detection.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- GET /data/raw/ from its FastAPI server
- GET /data/overall/ for aggregate scores
- Or read SQLite directly if available
- Use CLI Snapshot or HTTP scrape pattern

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_raw(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    benchmark_type TEXT,
    benchmark_name TEXT,
    value REAL,
    unit TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, benchmark_type, benchmark_name)
);

CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_overall(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_score REAL,
    cpu_score REAL,
    memory_score REAL,
    disk_score REAL,
    network_score REAL,
    subscores_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_history(
    machine_id TEXT,
    benchmark_date DATE,
    overall_score REAL,
    delta_from_baseline REAL,
    anomaly_detected BOOLEAN,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, benchmark_date)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement CloudBenchCollector with Collector trait
- Try HTTP endpoint first (if benchmarker server running)
- Fall back to SQLite direct read
- Calculate drift from baseline (first benchmark = baseline)
- Flag significant performance degradation (>10% from baseline)
- Handle missing benchmarker gracefully

UI VALUE:
- Machine performance baselines
- Performance drift alerts
- VPS provider comparison
- Capacity planning data

DRIFT DETECTION:
- Store first benchmark as baseline per machine
- Calculate delta on each collection
- Alert if delta > threshold (configurable, default 10%)
- Track trends over time

TESTS:
- Mock FastAPI responses
- Test SQLite fallback
- Verify drift calculation

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-new","title":"Phase 7: Scale + Governance + Integrations","description":"## Background\nThese additions extend vc beyond the current Phase 0-6 roadmap with scale, governance, and ecosystem integrations that are explicitly called out in the long-form plans but not yet represented in beads. They focus on data trustworthiness, incident replay, push-mode collection, external observability, GitHub correlation, and safer operator UX.\n\n## Goals\n- Add scale primitives (push-mode vc-node, offline sync) without disrupting pull-first MVP.\n- Improve data quality (freshness, drift detection, adaptive polling) so health/alerts are trustworthy.\n- Provide incident replay/time-travel to make incident management actionable.\n- Expand ecosystem integrations (Prometheus export, GitHub issues/PRs).\n- Add governance/contract tooling (schema docs, config lint, query guardrails, redaction).\n\n## Non-Goals\n- No replacement of upstream tools; vc remains an integration layer.\n- No breaking changes to existing schemas; use additive migrations only.\n- No mandatory SaaS dependencies; keep local-first.\n\n## Considerations\n- Must integrate with existing vc_store/collector/audit/alerts work.\n- All features require explicit unit tests + E2E scripts with structured logs per bd-30z.\n- Avoid cycles in deps; validate with bv.\n","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:48.421154816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:08:13.285674068Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-new.1","title":"Implement collector health + freshness + drift detection","description":"## Background
vc health and alerts are only trustworthy if we can quantify data freshness and detect drift from expected baselines. The long-form plans emphasize “stale data” badges and incident replay; this bead establishes the data-quality layer that all higher-level reasoning depends on.

## Goals
- Track per-collector freshness and success rate per machine.
- Detect baseline drift for machine profiles (CPU/mem/disk/net) and collector payloads.
- Surface data-quality signals to health score, alerts, and UI/robot outputs.
- Record provenance/lineage metadata to make explanations auditable.

## Implementation
### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE collector_health(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collector TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    success BOOLEAN,
    duration_ms BIGINT,
    rows_inserted BIGINT,
    bytes_parsed BIGINT,
    error_class TEXT,
    freshness_seconds BIGINT,
    payload_hash TEXT,
    collector_version TEXT,
    schema_version TEXT,
    cursor_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collector, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE machine_baselines(
    machine_id TEXT,
    baseline_window TEXT,       -- e.g. "7d" or "30d"
    computed_at TIMESTAMP,
    metrics_json TEXT,          -- mean/std/percentiles per metric
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, baseline_window)
);

CREATE TABLE drift_events(
    machine_id TEXT,
    detected_at TIMESTAMP,
    metric TEXT,
    current_value REAL,
    baseline_mean REAL,
    baseline_std REAL,
    z_score REAL,
    severity TEXT,
    evidence_json TEXT
);
```

### Logic
- Update `collector_health` from audit_events + collector runtime metadata.
- Freshness = now - last_successful_collect for that collector/machine.
- Baseline drift detection: rolling z-score or percentile divergence for core sys metrics.
- Emit `drift_events` when z-score exceeds configurable threshold.
- Add vc.toml config for freshness thresholds and drift sensitivity.

### Outputs
- Add staleness + drift summaries into health score inputs.
- Surface to TUI overview + robot health output as warnings.

## Risks
- False positives on drift during planned load spikes.
- Overhead from baseline computation if window too large.

## Success Criteria
- Staleness is visible in robot output and UI for each collector.
- Drift events are recorded and appear in incident evidence.
- Health score incorporates data freshness weighting.
- Lineage metadata allows tracing any alert back to inputs.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for freshness calculation and baseline stats (fixed seeds).
- Golden-data tests for drift detection on known time series.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_data_quality.sh` with JSON summary and log artifacts.
- Structured logs: collector, machine_id, freshness_seconds, drift_count.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:12.107058243Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:11.254659491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:12.106694788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Unit tests for freshness calculation and baseline stats (fixed seeds).
- Golden-data tests for drift detection on known time series.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_data_quality.sh` with JSON summary and log artifacts.
- Structured logs: collector, machine_id, freshness_seconds, drift_count.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T00:03:36.895191731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:11.254659491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:12.106694788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.10","title":"Implement config lint + wizard","description":"## Background
vc.toml will grow in complexity. Operators need validation, linting, and a guided wizard to prevent misconfiguration.

## Goals
- `vc config lint` that validates machines, SSH targets, and collector settings.
- `vc config wizard` to generate minimal config interactively or from templates.
- Emit actionable error messages with fixes.
- Provide non‑destructive diff preview when generating changes.

## Implementation
- Add JSON schema for config + custom validators (SSH, paths, tokens).
- Support `--fix` suggestions (non‑destructive).
- Validate machine tags and tool enablement coherence.
- Wizard writes to new file unless `--overwrite` is explicitly set.

## Risks
- Wizard must not overwrite existing config without explicit user action.

## Success Criteria
- Lint catches invalid configs and suggests fixes.
- Wizard can bootstrap a valid config from scratch.
- Diff preview shows exact changes before write.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for validators + schema rules.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/config/test_config_lint.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:54.133716478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:12:53.833369347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:12:53.833317158Z","close_reason":"Implemented config lint + wizard with:
- LintSeverity, LintIssue, LintSuggestion, LintResult types in vc_config
- lint() method on VcConfig with 15+ validation rules (errors, warnings, info)
- generate_default_toml() for config generation
- to_toml() for serialization
- CLI commands: vc config lint, vc config wizard, vc config show, vc config paths
- 11 new unit tests covering lint functionality
Pre-existing vc_oracle errors blocked full build verification but vc_config compiles and tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:54.133716478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:54.133716478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.10","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:25.573935691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:54.133716478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.10","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:54.133716478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.11","title":"Implement query guardrails + safe templates","description":"## Background
`vc query` is powerful but dangerous if used freely by agents. Guardrails and safe templates prevent expensive or unsafe queries.

## Goals
- Add a read‑only query sandbox with allowlisted templates.
- Enforce query timeouts + row limits.
- Provide explainability (why query allowed/denied).
- Ensure queries execute on a read‑only connection.

## Implementation
- `vc query --template <name> --params ...` for safe presets.
- Parse and reject non‑SELECT queries.
- Add max runtime and max output rows/bytes.
- Log query hashes + duration to audit_events.

## Risks
- Over‑restrictive guardrails could block legitimate use cases.

## Success Criteria
- Unsafe queries are rejected with clear reasons.
- Templates cover 80% of typical use.
- Read‑only enforcement is validated.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for query validator + template expansion.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_query_guardrails.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:01.267430595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:35:59.741152502Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:35:59.740667979Z","close_reason":"Query guardrails implemented - read-only validation, safe templates, SQL injection prevention, vc query CLI command","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:01.267430595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:01.267430595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.11","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:26.666677546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.11","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:01.267430595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.12","title":"Implement secrets/PII redaction pipeline","description":"## Background
Collectors ingest logs and artifacts that may contain secrets or PII. We need systematic redaction before storage and export.

## Goals
- Redact known secret patterns (tokens, keys) from raw_json/artifacts.
- Support per‑collector redaction rules.
- Preserve auditability while minimizing sensitive leakage.
- Provide rule versioning + test fixtures to avoid regressions.

## Implementation
- Redaction pipeline stage before insert into DuckDB.
- Configurable regex rules + allowlist for safe fields.
- Store redaction stats per batch.

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE redaction_events(
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    machine_id TEXT,
    collector TEXT,
    redacted_fields INTEGER,
    redacted_bytes INTEGER,
    sample_hash TEXT,
    rules_version TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT
);
```

## Risks
- Over‑redaction can reduce debugging value.
- Under‑redaction is a security risk.

## Success Criteria
- Secrets do not appear in stored artifacts for test fixtures.
- Redaction stats available for audits.
- Rule version changes are tracked.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for redaction regex rules.
- Fixture tests with seeded secrets.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/security/test_redaction.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:11.131141382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:28.512328612Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.12","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:11.131141382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:27.575103661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:11.131141382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:11.131141382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.12","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:28.511713503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.12","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:39:11.131141382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,56 @@
+-- Migration 011: collector_health, machine_baselines, drift_events
+-- Created: 2026-01-30
+-- Purpose: Track per-collector freshness, machine baselines, and metric drift
+
+-- Per-collector health tracking
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collector_health (
+    machine_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collector TEXT NOT NULL,
+    collected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
+    success BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
+    duration_ms BIGINT,
+    rows_inserted BIGINT DEFAULT 0,
+    bytes_parsed BIGINT DEFAULT 0,
+    error_class TEXT,
+    freshness_seconds BIGINT,
+    payload_hash TEXT,
+    collector_version TEXT,
+    schema_version TEXT,
+    cursor_json TEXT,
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collector, collected_at)
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_collector_health_machine
+    ON collector_health(machine_id, collector);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_collector_health_ts
+    ON collector_health(collected_at);
+
+-- Machine baseline profiles (rolling stats)
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS machine_baselines (
+    machine_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    baseline_window TEXT NOT NULL,
+    computed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT current_timestamp,
+    metrics_json TEXT NOT NULL,
+    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, baseline_window)
+);
+
+-- Drift events detected by z-score or percentile divergence
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS drift_events (
+    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+    machine_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    detected_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT current_timestamp,
+    metric TEXT NOT NULL,
+    current_value DOUBLE NOT NULL,
+    baseline_mean DOUBLE NOT NULL,
+    baseline_std DOUBLE NOT NULL,
+    z_score DOUBLE NOT NULL,
+    severity TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info',
+    evidence_json TEXT
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_drift_events_machine
+    ON drift_events(machine_id);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_drift_events_ts
+    ON drift_events(detected_at);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_drift_events_severity
+    ON drift_events(severity);
